subject.index_group: MSCI regional equities and Asian technology sub-gauge

value.prior_session_context: closed at a record high on 2026-06-22
value.basis: late-session regional snapshot reported by The Business Times/Bloomberg on 2026-06-23

market_session: Asia session on 2026-06-23; regional late-session snapshot, not final close for every component market
text: Asian stocks slumped on June 23 as MSCI's regional equity gauge declined more than 2% after closing at a record high the prior day, while an Asian technology sub-gauge snapped an eight-day winning run.

value.halt_trigger: KOSPI down more than 8% from previous session close
value.record_context: fourth time this year and 10th time on record that KRX halted all stocks for 20 minutes

market_session: South Korea regular equity session close on 2026-06-23
text: South Korea's KOSPI plunged 910.71 points, or 9.99%, to close at 8,203.84 on June 23 after hitting an intraday high of 9,175.45; the Korea Exchange triggered a market-wide circuit breaker around 2:33 p.m. local time after the index dropped more than 8%, halting all stocks for 20 minutes.

market_session: South Korea regular equity session close on 2026-06-23; won quote as of 15:30 KST
text: Korea's selloff was chip-led: Samsung Electronics fell 12.31% and SK Hynix fell 12.47%, with UPI/Yonhap reporting foreign and institutional net selling of 4.13 trillion won and 4.55 trillion won against 8.58 trillion won of retail net buying. Anadolu also reported decliners outnumbering gainers 856 to 46, turnover of 59.9 trillion won, Samsung Electro-Mechanics down 9.6%, Hyundai Motor down 12.05%, LG Energy Solution down 6.1%, and the won at 1,539.1 per dollar as of 3:30 p.m.

value.basis: Jiji Press reported closes and point changes at 2026-06-23 15:30 JST; percentages are calculated from reported close and point move

market_session: Japan regular equity session close on 2026-06-23

calculation.method: implied percent move = reported point change divided by prior close inferred as reported close minus reported point change; Nikkei: -2565.58/(69788.38+2565.58); TOPIX: -104.67/(3990.38+104.67)

text: Japan sold off at the June 23 Tokyo close: Jiji Press reported the Nikkei Average at 69,788.38, down 2,565.58 points, and TOPIX at 3,990.38, down 104.67 points. Those point moves imply declines of about 3.55% and 2.56%, respectively, versus the prior close.

value.driver: rotation out of AI and semiconductor winners after an extended rally
value.business_times_context: regional tech sub-gauge snapped an eight-day winning run and Micron earnings were described as a key upcoming test for the AI spending rally
value.reuters_context: global stocks were dragged lower by a broad technology and semiconductor selloff as investors took profits on a long rally
value.korea_link: KOSPI decline centered on heavy chipweights Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix

market_session: Asia session and later global session on 2026-06-23
text: The Asia move was driven by AI and semiconductor profit-taking rather than a new broad macro shock: The Business Times reported rotation out of this year's best-performing technology shares and an Asian technology sub-gauge ending an eight-day winning run, while Reuters described a global selloff in technology and semiconductor shares as investors took profits on a long rally.

subject.cross_asset_theme: oil relief versus equity tech selloff

value.trading_economics_basis: CFD reference that tracks the benchmark market, not official ICE settlement
value.reuters_context: oil remained below $80 as Strait of Hormuz tanker traffic increased and physical market prices neared pre-conflict levels

as_of: 2026-06-23 (Trading Economics daily CFD reference; table timestamp 14:01 shown without timezone)
market_session: Global commodity trading on 2026-06-23; CFD reference basis, not official Brent settlement
text: Oil provided cross-asset relief but did not stop the Asia equity unwind: Trading Economics put Brent at 76.92 USD/Bbl on June 23, down 1.26% on the day and 20.43% over the month, while Reuters reported oil below $80 as tanker traffic through the Strait of Hormuz increased and physical market prices neared pre-conflict levels.
